channel.title: AlloyDB on MCP Toolbox for Databases
channel.description: Recent content in AlloyDB on MCP Toolbox for Databases

item.title: Quickstart (MCP with AlloyDB)
item.description: Overview Model Context Protocol is an open protocol that standardizes how applications provide context to LLMs. Check out this page on how to connect to Toolbox via MCP. Before you begin This guide assumes you have already done the following: Create a AlloyDB cluster and instance with a database and user. Connect to the instance using AlloyDB Studio, psql command-line tool, or any other PostgreSQL client. Enable the pgvector and google_ml_integration extensions.

item.title: Getting started with alloydb-ai-nl tool
item.description: Objective In this notebook, we will be using the AlloyDB AI NL tool to generate and execute SQL queries on AlloyDB databases. We will start by setting up an AlloyDB database and then connecting to it using MCP Toolbox and ADK. This allows us to leverage AlloyDB NL to SQL capabilities in agentic applications. For detailed information on the AlloyDB capability, please see AlloyDB AI Natural Language Overview. Before you Begin Complete the before you begin setup from Use AlloyDB AI natural language to generate SQL.
